chapter_title.en: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions

text: 3586 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَخِيهِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلنُّثَارِ مِنَ اَلسُّكَّرِ وَ اَللَّوْزِ وَ أَشْبَاهِهِ أَ يَحِلُّ أَكْلُهُ فَقَالَ "يُكْرَهُ كُلُّ مَالٍ يُنْتَهَبُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3586 - It is narrated from Ali ibn Ja'far, from his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
"I asked him about scattered treats such as sugar, almonds, and similar items-whether it is permissible to eat them.
Imam (as) replied: 'It is disliked to consume anything that is seized unlawfully.'"
